title: Light - Reflection and Refraction
introduction: This lesson focuses on the properties of light, specifically reflection and refraction. Reflection is the bouncing back of light from a surface, while refraction is the bending of light as it passes through a medium with different optical densities.
visual_description: Imagine a ray of light hitting a mirror (reflection) or passing through a prism (refraction).

sections.heading: Reflection
sections.content: Laws of reflection, formation of image in plane and spherical mirrors, and the concept of virtual and real images.

sections.heading: Refraction
sections.content: Understanding the refractive index, Snell's law, and the formation of images in lenses.

activity: Conduct an experiment to observe reflection using a concave mirror. Place an object at different distances from the mirror and observe the size and position of the reflected image.
summary: Understanding reflection and refraction helps us appreciate how light behaves in our daily lives, from seeing our reflection in mirrors to viewing objects underwater through a snorkel.
key_facts: The angle of incidence equals the angle of reflection for light rays hitting a smooth surface.
The speed of light changes when it passes through different media, causing refraction.
Snell's law describes how the angles of incidence and refraction relate to each other and the refractive indices of the two media.
